---
title: "Protocols"
subtitle: "Field methods, manuals and compatible monitoring workflows for Rede C2 and partner networks"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: false
  freeze: auto
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@700;900&family=Lora:wght@400;500;700&family=Space+Mono:wght@400;700&display=swap');

body {
  background-image: url("../figures/c2_bg.jpg");
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  background-size: cover;
  background-attachment: fixed;
  background-position: center top;
  font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
}

main.content {
  background: transparent;
  padding-top: 2rem;
  padding-bottom: 3rem;
}

.quarto-title-block {
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
}

.quarto-title-block .quarto-title {
  background: rgba(250, 246, 238, 0.94);
  border-top: 4px solid #5C7A3E;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(200,90,58,0.22);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
  padding: 1.1rem 1.2rem;
}

.quarto-title-block .title {
  color: #C85A3A;
  font-weight: 900;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
}

.quarto-title-block .subtitle {
  color: #4A3A22;
  font-style: italic;
  margin: 0;
}

.logo-wrap {
  text-align: right;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.logo-wrap img {
  width: 180px;
  max-width: 32vw;
}

.image-strip img {
  width: 100%;
  height: 220px;
  object-fit: cover;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 22px rgba(0,0,0,0.16);
}

.card-box {
  background: rgba(250, 246, 238, 0.96);
  border-radius: 18px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.16);
  border-top: 3px solid #C85A3A;
  padding: 1.6rem 1.4rem;
  margin: 0 0 1.4rem 0;
}

.badges {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.65rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.badge {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 0.34rem 0.8rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
}

.badge-caatinga {
  background: rgba(200,90,58,0.10);
  color: #C85A3A;
  border: 1px solid rgba(200,90,58,0.30);
}

.badge-cerrado {
  background: rgba(92,122,62,0.10);
  color: #4A7030;
  border: 1px solid rgba(92,122,62,0.30);
}

.badge-network {
  background: rgba(139,105,20,0.10);
  color: #7A5A18;
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,105,20,0.30);
}

.intro-lead {
  font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  font-style: italic;
  font-size: 1.2rem;
  color: #2A1F10;
  line-height: 1.7;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
  padding-left: 1rem;
  border-left: 3px solid #5C7A3E;
}

.note-box {
  background: rgba(92,122,62,0.08);
  border-left: 4px solid #5C7A3E;
  padding: 0.9rem 1rem;
  border-radius: 10px;
  color: #364426;
  margin-top: 1rem;
}

.section-title {
  font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  color: #C85A3A;
  font-size: 1.7rem;
  margin-top: 0;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
  padding-bottom: 0.45rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #DDD0B3;
}

.proto-card {
  background: rgba(221,208,179,0.22);
  border: 1px solid #DDD0B3;
  border-radius: 14px;
  padding: 1rem;
  height: 100%;
}

.proto-card h3 {
  font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  font-size: 1.12rem;
  color: #2A1F10;
  margin-top: 0;
  margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
}

.proto-subtitle {
  font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: #7A5A18;
  margin-bottom: 0.65rem;
}

.proto-card p {
  color: #4A3A22;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.pill-links a {
  display: inline-block;
  margin: 0.25rem 0.35rem 0.25rem 0;
  padding: 0.32rem 0.72rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  text-decoration: none;
  border: 1px solid rgba(200,90,58,0.25);
  background: rgba(200,90,58,0.08);
  color: #C85A3A;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .logo-wrap {
    text-align: center;
  }

  .logo-wrap img {
    width: 140px;
  }

  .image-strip img {
    height: 170px;
  }
}
</style>

::: {.logo-wrap}
![](../figures/logoc2.png){alt="Rede C2 logo"}
:::

::: {.card-box}

::: {.badges}
<span class="badge badge-caatinga">Caatinga</span>
<span class="badge badge-cerrado">Cerrado</span>
<span class="badge badge-network">Protocols</span>
:::

::: {.intro-lead}
Standardized protocols are the backbone of long-term monitoring: they keep data comparable across sites, teams and years.
:::

Rede C2 works with permanent plots in dry ecosystems and connects with broader monitoring initiatives. This page gathers core protocol references that can support plot establishment, remeasurement, field standardization, data harmonization and cross-network comparability.

::: {.note-box}
The DRYFLOR field manual is especially relevant for Rede C2 because it offers a standardized workflow for permanent plots in seasonally dry tropical forests, including plot layout, tagging, remeasurement and woody stem monitoring.
:::

:::

::: {.card-box}

## Core protocol references { .section-title }

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Rede C2

::: {.proto-subtitle}
Network base
:::

Space for Rede C2’s own protocols, field sheets and coding standards for plot installation, recensus, disturbance records, traits and soils.

::: {.pill-links}
[Plot protocol](plot-establishment.html)
[Datasheets](datasheets.html)
[Field codes](field-codes.html)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### DRYFLOR field manual

::: {.proto-subtitle}
Dry forest permanent plots
:::

A key manual for establishing and remeasuring permanent plots in seasonally dry tropical forests. It is especially useful for woody stems, tagging rules and long-term comparability.

::: {.pill-links}
[Protocols page](https://www.dryflor.info/publications/protocols)
[Português](https://doi.org/10.5521/forestplots.net/2020_4c)
[English](https://www.dryflor.info/files/Protocol_v1.2_English.pdf)
[Español](https://www.dryflor.info/files/Protocol_v1.2_Spanish.pdf)
:::
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### ForestPlots.net

::: {.proto-subtitle}
Field resources and manuals
:::

ForestPlots.net provides field resources, publications and practical material that support tropical plot monitoring and interoperability across networks.

::: {.pill-links}
[No campo](https://forestplots.net/pt/utilizando-o-forestplots/no-campo)
[Publications](https://forestplots.net/en/publications)
[Download](https://forestplots.net/pt/utilizando-o-forestplots/download)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### SECO

::: {.proto-subtitle}
Dry tropics compatibility
:::

SECO connects dry tropics monitoring initiatives and provides protocol pages and modules that align with DryFlor, ForestPlots and related networks.

::: {.pill-links}
[Protocols page](https://blogs.ed.ac.uk/seco-project/science/protocols/)
[Project page](https://blogs.ed.ac.uk/seco-project/)
[Network connections](https://blogs.ed.ac.uk/seco-project/network-connections/)
:::
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### SEOSAW

::: {.proto-subtitle}
Linked woodland modules
:::

SEOSAW offers interoperable protocols and datasheets for woodland monitoring, useful as a methodological reference for compatible modules and workflow design.

::: {.pill-links}
[Protocols](https://seosaw.github.io/manuals.html)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Recommended citation anchor

::: {.proto-subtitle}
DRYFLOR Portuguese manual
:::

This DOI can be used as a stable citation anchor when referring to the Portuguese DRYFLOR field manual in Rede C2 methods and protocol references.

::: {.pill-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.5521/forestplots.net/2020_4c)
:::
:::
:::
::::

:::

::: {.card-box}

## Suggested Rede C2 sections { .section-title }

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Plot establishment and remeasurement

Plot geometry, subplot layout, coordinates, tagging, recensus intervals and replacement rules.

::: {.pill-links}
[Open](plot-establishment.html)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Woody stems census

Diameter standards, multiple stems, damage codes, recruits, mortality and field note structure.

::: {.pill-links}
[Open](woody-census.html)
:::
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Herb layer and regeneration

Modules for herbs, seedlings and regeneration tracking compatible with dry-forest and savanna workflows.

::: {.pill-links}
[Open](herb-layer.html)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.proto-card}
### Soils, traits and disturbance

Optional compatible modules inspired by partner networks to maximize interoperability and ecological interpretation.

::: {.pill-links}
[Soils](soils.html)
[Traits](traits.html)
[Disturbance](disturbance.html)
:::
:::
:::
::::
:::
